channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Kings make 22 three-pointers, blow out Jazz, 141-97
item.description: SACRAMENTO (ABC4 Sports) - On Friday, the Utah Jazz put up 141 points in a blowout win over Portland. On Sunday, they were on the receiving end of a 141-point outburst. Kevin Huerter had 26 points, including six 3-pointers, and the Sacramento Kings beat the Utah Jazz 141-97 on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Juan Soto agrees to record $765 million, 15-year contract with Mets, AP source says
item.description: A person familiar with the deal tells The Associated Press that star outfielder Juan Soto and the New York Mets have agreed to a record $765 million, 15-year contract.
item.dc:creator: Ronald Blum, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Juan Soto agrees to MLB-record $765 million deal with Mets: reports
item.description: Juan Soto has landed in Queens, as the New York Mets reportedly dished out an MLB-record $765 million contract over 15 years, smashing Shohei Ohtani's deal from last offseason.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Allen sets NFL mark with 3 TD passes and 3 TD runs, but Stafford's Rams hold off Bills 44-42
item.description: Matthew Stafford and the Los Angeles Rams had to pile up a veritable mountain of points, yards and time-of-possession advantage early on to give themselves even a chance against the Buffalo Bills' ferocious fourth-quarter rally and a historic six-touchdown performance by Josh Allen.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dave Parker and Dick Allen elected to baseball's Hall of Fame
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Having waited for the call from Cooperstown for nearly three decades, Dave Parker burst into tears Sunday when he was elected to baseball’s Hall of Fame along with the late Dick Allen. “Yeah, I cried,” Parker said after receiving the news from Hall chair Jane Forbes Clark.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: College Football Playoff's first 12-team bracket is set with Oregon No. 1 and SMU in, Alabama out
item.description: The new, 12-team College Football Playoff brings with it a promise to be bigger, more exciting, more lucrative. Perfect or 100% fair? Well, nobody ever believed that.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Brayden Point's 4-point night leads the Lightning over the Canucks 4-2
item.description: Brayden Point scored twice and added two assists, and the Tampa Bay Lightning edged the Vancouver Canucks 4-2 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Darnold delivers for Vikings with career-high 347 yards and 5 TDs to beat Falcons, Cousins 42-21
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Sam Darnold tossed aside his stoic demeanor for a moment after realizing he was on the videoboard, aggressively twirling a towel to further stoke the crowd's fire after the Minnesota Vikings had pulled away from Kirk Cousins and the Atlanta Falcons late in the game.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scottie Scheffler ends his big year in the Bahamas with his 9th victory
item.description:
Scottie Scheffler returned from a two-month break and nothing changed. He won the Hero World Challenge on Sunday with a 9-under 63 to tie the tournament record at Albany Golf Club and win by six shots.
Scheffler, the first player since Tiger Woods in 2009 to spend an entire calendar year at No.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Baker Mayfield throws for 3 TDs, Bucs take over 1st in NFC South with 28-13 win over Raiders
item.description: Baker Mayfield threw for 295 yards and three touchdowns, helping the Tampa Bay Buccaneers beat the Las Vegas Raiders 28-13 and take over sole possession of first place in the NFC South on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: FRED GOODALL AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Blackhawks starting goaltender Petr Mrazek placed on injured reserve with groin injury
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Chicago Blackhawks placed goaltender Petr Mrazek on injured reserve on Sunday with a left groin injury. Mrazek was replaced by Arvid Soderblom in the first period of Saturday's 4-2 loss to Winnipeg. The 32-year-old Mrazek is 7-11-1 with a 2.83 goals-against average and .
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Nick Saban questions strength of schedule factor in CFP as Alabama misses field
item.description: Former Alabama Crimson Tide head coach Nick Saban wondered if strength of schedule was really a factor when it came to the College Football Playoff.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lindsey Vonn is encouraged by how close she is to being competitive in ski racing return at age 40
item.description: COPPER MOUNTAIN, Colo. (AP) — Lindsey Vonn is still getting her ski equipment dialed in and getting used to going full speed again on her new titanium knee.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alexandre Pantoja successfully defends flyweight belt with technical submission of Kai Asakura
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Alexandre Pantoja staked his claim as one of the greatest flyweights in UFC history when he defended his title Saturday night with a technical submission of Japan's Kai Asakura, using a rear naked choke to put his challenger to sleep at the 2:05 mark of the second round.
item.dc:creator: W.G. RAMIREZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 18 Clemson beats No. 8 SMU 34-31 on last-second field goal for ACC title, berth in the CFP
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Cade Klubnik sat in his car for more than hour last Saturday crying following Clemson's loss to rival South Carolina, figuring his team's chances of reaching the College Football Playoff were over. My how things can change.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 1 Oregon staves off valiant Penn State effort to win Big Ten title
item.description: The Oregon Ducks moved to 13-0 by winning the Big Ten championship game over Penn State on Saturday, ensuring a first-round bye in the playoff.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utes drop first home game to St. Mary's, 72-63
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Runnin' Utes had won its first six home games by an average margin of 32 points. But they hadn't played a team as talented as Saint Mary's. The Gaels came into the Huntsman Center Saturday, and led by Paulius Murauskas, beat the utes, 72-63.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Willy Adames agrees to $182 million, 7-year deal with the Giants, AP source says
item.description: Willy Adames has agreed to a $182 million, seven-year contract with San Francisco, providing the Giants with a power-hitting shortstop in the prime of his career, according to a person familiar with the negotiations.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Jazz cruise to rare blowout win over Portland, 141-99
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - In what has been an extremely difficult rebuilding year, the Utah Jazz will relish nights like this one.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Manny Pacquiao, winner of titles in a record 8 weight classes, elected to Boxing Hall of Fame
item.description: (AP) -- Manny Pacquiao, who won titles in a record eight weight classes from flyweight to super welterweight while becoming one of boxing's biggest draws, was elected Thursday to the International Boxing Hall of Fame.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Batty, Ferrin named to All-Big 12 first team
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Three Cougars were named first-team All-Big 12 including defensive end Tyler Batty, offensive left tackle Caleb Etienne and kicker Will Ferrin. In addition, senior defensive back Jakob Robinson and junior kick returner Keelan Marion earned All-Big 12 Second Team honors.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Kuithe, Fano earn All-Big 12 first team honors
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Despite missing the last three games of the season, Utes tight end Brant Kuithe was named to the All-Big 12 First Team on Wednesday. Kuithe joins Utes offensive lineman Spencer Fano as the only Utes to earn first team honors.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Golden State Valkyries unveil uniforms ahead of inaugural WNBA season
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The Golden State Valkyries won’t take the court for their inaugural 2025 season until May 16, but fans are getting an early look at the WNBA team’s uniforms which were unveiled Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, KRON4

item.title: Nick Rimando elected to National Soccer Hall of Fame
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Former Real Salt Lake goalkeeper Nick Rimando has been elected to the National Soccer Hall of Fame. Rimando will be enshrined with the Class of 2025 on May 3, 2025 in Frisco, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: BYU runners use olive oil to win NCAA championship
item.description: The school's coach said his runners used “an old-school running trick” by rubbing olive oil on their legs to keep themselves warm.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: McCaffrey injury potentially rules him out for rest of season
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — San Francisco running back Christian McCaffrey suffered a knee injury that coach Kyle Shanahan described as “potentially” season-ending in the 49ers’ 35-10 loss to the Buffalo Bills on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4
